name: Blender
title: Blender 3D & CGI Fundamentals
subtitle: From cube to render — the open-source 3D pipeline used by indies and studios.
summary: Blender is the open-source 3D powerhouse — used for indie films, AAA game cinematics, Pixar-quality renders, motion graphics, product visualization, and architecture. This course covers the entire 3D pipeline: modeling (polygon + sculpting), materials and shaders, lighting and cameras, rendering (Cycles + Eevee), animation and rigging, and modern tools like Geometry Nodes. Practical workflow from concept to final image.

modules.title: Blender interface and modeling foundations
modules.objective: Navigate Blender; build clean polygon models.

modules.title: Materials, lighting, and cameras
modules.objective: Make objects look real or stylized.

modules.title: Rendering — Cycles and Eevee
modules.objective: Convert your 3D scene to a final image with the right engine.

lessons.title: Cycles vs. Eevee — when to use which

modules.title: Animation, rigging, and simulation
modules.objective: Bring scenes to life with motion.

lessons.title: Keyframe animation and the Graph Editor

modules.title: Geometry Nodes, sculpting, and the modern Blender pipeline
modules.objective: Use Blender's procedural and high-detail workflows.

lessons.title: Asset management and the asset library
Blender in production — workflow and pipeline
